completed: Drafted a proof-review artifact for issue #4 in docs/omitted_context_mixture_lemma.md
Identified that the direct mixture identity uses theta conditional on X and Z unless trial design exogeneity or a fixed-design convention is stated
Recorded proposed assumption A-006 and marked claim C-002 as in progress rather than accepted
why_it_matters: The mixture lemma is the first theorem object in the OCECM proof route.
Surfacing the X/Z conditioning issue prevents the manuscript from overclaiming a simplified marginal law before PI approval.

checks_run.name: research update validator
checks_run.notes: Ran python3 scripts/validate_research_update.py on this update and make validate-update on the example update.

decision_needed.question: For issue #4, should the theorem package approve A-006 so the main display uses F_{theta|Z}, or should it keep the more general F_{theta|X,Z} mixture as the primary statement?
options: Approve A-006 and use the simplified F_{theta|Z} display where trial design is fixed or randomized.
Do not approve A-006; make F_{theta|X,Z} the primary theorem statement and mention F_{theta|Z} only as a corollary.
decision_needed.default_if_no_response: Do not promote C-002 beyond in_progress and keep the proof draft out of the manuscript.

risks_or_uncertainties: The proof draft has not yet received proof-critic review.
The simplified issue formula can fail under adaptive or stratified trial assignment.
next_bounded_task: Run proof-critic review on Lemma 1.1 and prepare the PI-selected theorem statement for manuscript or supplement insertion.
